1.1: Paulus and Sylvanus and Timotheus to the church of Thessaloniqa in God The Father and in our Lord Yeshua The Messiah. Grace be with you and peace.
1.2: We thank God always for all of you, and we are reminded of you in our prayers constantly.
1.3: And we recall before God The Father the works of your faith, the labor of your love and patience of your hope, which are in our Lord Yeshua The Messiah.
1.4: For we know your election, brethren, beloved of God,
1.5: Because our evangelism was not in words only, but it was to you also in power and in The Spirit of Holiness and in true conviction. You also know how we have been among you for your sakes.
1.6: And you imitated us and Our Lord, for you received the word in great affliction and in the joy of The Spirit of Holiness.
1.7: And you have been a model to all believers who are in Macedonia and in Akaia.
1.8: For the word of our Lord has been heard from you, not only in Maqedonia and in Akaia, but in every place your faith in God is heard, so that we will not need to say anything about you.
1.9: For they relate what an introduction we had to you and how you were turned to God from the worship of idols to serve The Living and True God,
1.10: While you await his Son from Heaven, Yeshua, him whom he raised from among the dead; He is The One who delivers us from the fury that is coming.

2.1: And you know, my brethren, that our introduction to you has not been for nothing.
2.2: But first we suffered and we were abused in Philippus as you know, and then in a great struggle we spoke The Gospel of The Messiah with you in the boldness of our God.
2.3: For our comfort was not from deception nor from impurity, nor by treachery.
2.4: But as when we were tested by God to be entrusted with his Gospel, so we speak, not as if we would please the children of men, but God who tests our hearts.
2.5: For neither do we ever employ enticing speech as you know, neither an occasion for greed, God is witness,
2.6: Neither do we seek glory from the children of men; not from you, neither from others, when we could have been honored as Apostles of The Messiah.
2.7: But we were meek among you, even as a nursemaid who cherishes her children.
2.8: In this way also we cherish and long to give you, not only The Good News of God, but our lives also, because you are our beloved.
2.9: For you remember, brethren, that we were toiling and laboring in the works of our hands by night. And by day, that we might not be a burden to anyone of you,
2.10: You and God are witnesses, how we preached The Good News of God to you purely and rightly, and we were without a fault toward all the believers.
2.11: Just as you know that we have been like a father to each one of you, pleading with his children, and we comfort your hearts and charge you
2.12: To walk as it is suitable to God, who has called you into his Kingdom and into his glory.
2.13: Because of this, we also thank God constantly that you have received the word of God from us, not as if you received the word of the children of men, but just as it is truly the word of God, and which is working among you by deeds in those who believe.
2.14: But you, my brethren, imitated the churches of God that are in Judea, these who are in Yeshua The Messiah, so that you suffered also by the children of your nation just as they did also of the Judeans,
2.15: Those who murdered Yeshua The Messiah, and they have persecuted their own Prophets, and us, and they do not please God and have become opposed to all people.
2.16: For they forbid us to speak with the Gentiles, that they may live to fulfill their sins always, but fury has come upon them until the end.
2.17: But we, brethren, were bereaved for a short time of your presence, but not in our hearts, and we took all the more pains in much love to see your faces.
2.18: And we wished to come to you, I Paul, once and twice, and Satan hindered me.
2.19: For what is our hope and our joy and the crown of our glory, but you alone, before our Lord Yeshua at his coming?
2.20: For you are our glory and our joy.

3.1: And because we did not resist, we chose to remain in Athens by ourselves,
3.2: And we sent Timotheus our brother to you, a Minister of God and our helper in The Good News of The Messiah, that he might strengthen you and inquire of you concerning your faith,
3.3: Lest any of you should lose hope by such sufferings, for you are aware that we are appointed to this.
3.4: For also when we were with you, we said to you before that we would be persecuted, just as you know it happened.
3.5: Because of this I also did not resist until I sent to know your faith, lest the tempter would tempt you and our labor would be worthless.
3.6: But now since Timotheus has come to us from your midst and he gave us good news concerning your faith and about your love, and that you have a good commemoration of us in every place and you desire to see us, just as we also do you,
3.7: Therefore we were comforted by you, brethren, concerning all our adversities and in our afflictions, because of your faith.
3.8: And now we live, if you will abide in Our Lord.
3.9: For what thanks can we pay on your behalf to God, for every joy with which we rejoice because of you,
3.10: Except that before God, we pray all the more earnestly, night and day, to see your faces, and perfect whatever is lacking in your faith?
3.11: But God Our Father and our Lord Yeshua The Messiah shall direct our way to you,
3.12: And may he multiply and increase your love one toward another, and that to every person, just as we love you,
3.13: And establish your hearts without a fault in holiness before God Our Father at the arrival of our Lord Yeshua The Messiah with all of his Holy Ones.

4.1: Therefore, my brethren, we beg of you and pray earnestly for you in our Lord Yeshua that as you have received of us how you must walk and please God, that you may grow all the more.
4.2: For you know those commandments which we gave to you by our Lord Yeshua.
4.3: For this is the will of God: your holiness, and that you be separate from all fornication,
4.4: And that each one of you know to possess his vessel in holiness and in honor,
4.5: And not in the passion of lust, as others of the Gentiles who do not know God;
4.6: Neither should you presume to violate and take advantage, anyone, of his brother in this matter, because our Lord is the avenger of all such, just as we have said before and testified to you,
4.7: For God has not called you to impurity but to holiness.
4.8: Therefore, whoever defrauds, does not defraud a man, but God, he who gives his Spirit of Holiness among you.
4.9: But you do not need to have written to you concerning love of the brethren, for you yourselves are taught by God to love one another.
4.10: You also are doing so to all the brethren who are in all Macedonia, but I beg of you, my brethren, that you superabound,
4.11: And that you would be diligent, peaceable and occupied with your business, working with your hands just as we commanded you,
4.12: That you would be walking in good form toward outsiders and that you would be dependent on no man.
4.13: I want you to know my brethren, that you should not have sorrow for those who are asleep, as do the rest of mankind who have no hope.
4.14: For if we believe that Yeshua died and arose, in this way also God shall bring with him those who have fallen asleep in Yeshua.
4.15: But this we say to you by the word of Our Lord, that we, those who remain at the coming of Our Lord, we who have life, shall not overtake those who are asleep;
4.16: Because our Lord shall descend from Heaven with a command and with the voice of the Archangel and with the trumpet blast of God, and the dead who are in The Messiah shall rise first;
4.17: And then we who remain, who have life, we shall be carried away with them together in clouds to a meeting of our Lord in the air, and in this way, we shall always be with Our Lord.
4.18: Therefore, comfort one another with these words.

5.1: But you do not need to be written to about times and seasons, my brethren,
5.2: For you truly know that the day of our Lord so comes as a thief in the night.
5.3: When they shall say, “There is peace and quiet”, then suddenly destruction shall arise upon them as labor pains upon a pregnant woman, and they shall not escape.
5.4: But you, my brethren, you are not in darkness that the day should overtake you as a thief.
5.5: For you are all children of light and children of the day and you are not children of the night, neither children of darkness.
5.6: Let us not sleep therefore as the rest, but let us be watchful and sensible.
5.7: For those who sleep, sleep at night, and those who are drunk are drunk at night.
5.8: But we are the children of the day; let us be vigilant in our minds and put on the breastplate of faith and of love and let us take the helmet of the hope of Life,
5.9: Because God has not appointed us to wrath, but to the possession of Life in our Lord Yeshua The Messiah,
5.10: He who died for our sake, that whether we are awake or asleep, we shall live together with him.
5.11: Because of this, comfort one another and build one another up just as you have done.
5.12: But we beg of you, my brethren, that you will recognize those who labor among you and have standing among you in our Lord and teach you,
5.13: That they should be esteemed by you in greater love, and be at peace with them because of their works.
5.14: But we beg of you, my brethren, correct wrongdoers, encourage the feeble souls, bear the burdens of the weak and be patient with every person.
5.15: Beware lest anyone of you reward evil for evil, but always run after the good with each and every person.
5.18: Give thanks in everything, for this is the will of God in Yeshua The Messiah among you.
5.21: Explore everything and hold what is excellent.
5.23: But The God of peace shall make all of you perfectly holy and shall keep your whole spirit, soul and body without fault for the arrival of our Lord Yeshua The Messiah.
5.24: Faithful is he who has called you; it is he who shall perform it.
5.26: Invoke the peace of all our brethren with a holy kiss.
5.27: I bind you with an oath by Our Lord, that this letter will be read to all the holy brethren.
5.28: The grace of our Lord Yeshua The Messiah be with you. Amen.
